questions.prompt: function identity<T>(value: T): T — the T here is called:
options: A type parameter
A template literal
A constraint
A utility type
questions.explanation: T is a generic type parameter — a placeholder that gets filled with a real type when the function is called.

options: All User properties are required
All User properties are optional
All User properties are readonly
User properties become nulls
questions.explanation: Partial<T> makes every property in T optional (adds ? to each key).

options: All members to have the same properties
Each member to have a shared literal-typed discriminant property
The union to be created with the & operator
All members to extend a base interface
questions.explanation: The discriminant is a shared property with a unique literal type per member (like status: 'loading' | 'success' | 'error').

options: Only the id and createdAt fields
User without the id and createdAt fields
The type of id and createdAt combined
User where id and createdAt are optional
questions.explanation: Omit<T, K> creates a new type with all properties of T except those in K.

questions.prompt: How do you make TypeScript error if a switch/case doesn't handle all union members?
options: Add noImplicitReturns to tsconfig
In the default case, assign the value to a never-typed variable
Use exhaustive: true in tsconfig
TypeScript does this automatically with union types
questions.explanation: default: const _: never = value; — if a union member is unhandled, TypeScript errors because it's assignable to the type, not never.

questions.prompt: What is a type predicate and how is it different from a plain boolean check?
questions.answer_key: A type predicate is a return type annotation 'val is User' on a function. It tells TypeScript to narrow val's type in the if-true branch. A plain boolean check doesn't affect TypeScript's type narrowing — it's just a runtime condition.

questions.prompt: What does <T extends { name: string }> mean?
options: T must be a subclass of { name: string }
T is constrained to only types that have a name: string property
T defaults to { name: string }
T is replaced by { name: string } at compile time
questions.explanation: extends in a generic constraint limits the allowed types to those that satisfy the given shape (structural typing).
